probe: flat-band-at-E_F alignment via QE tot_charge POSITIVE jellium (hole doping = remove electrons)
gate: |deltaE|<0.10 eV AND m<0.5 uB -> promote; deltaE>0.2 OR m>0.5 -> falsify
method: QE 7.5 pw.x scf(nspin=2) + bands(Gamma-K-M-Gamma-A) + bands.x; PBE ONCV SG15; ecutwfc=90 ecutrho=360 Ry; MP smearing degauss=0.02; tot_charge jellium background. Flat-band deltaE = (kagome flat band, band-44, k-mean) - E_F via validated parser parse_flatband_dE.py (control reproduces known CoSn deltaE=-0.445 to 4 decimals).
host: summer (local pool, RTX5070 box, free) -np 6; lean per-point out/ cleanup (disk-bound)
finding: Hole doping lowers E_F (~-0.06 eV per +0.2 holes) BUT the kagome flat band drops FASTER, so deltaE = E_flat - E_F goes MORE negative (-0.445 -> -0.544). Flat band moves AWAY from E_F, not toward it. Slope ~-0.165 eV/hole. Combined with the electron-doped scan (deltaE -0.445 -> -0.585, slope -0.23 eV/e-), BOTH rigid-doping directions push the CoSn flat band deeper. Additionally hole doping monotonically wakes competing magnetism (m 0.09 -> 0.63, crossing the 0.5 gate near +0.45 holes).
why: In CoSn the kagome flat band is tied to the Co-3d manifold; rigid-band carrier doping shifts E_F but the d-derived flat band tracks with it (and deeper), so doping cannot independently bring the flat band onto E_F. The 'dope CoSn to E_F' axis is closed on BOTH dials.
rules_out: the entire rigid-band-doping route to flat-band-at-E_F on CoSn-type kagome (electron AND hole).
controls: Dp=0 control reproduces the undoped CoSn deltaE=-0.445 and m=0.09 -> setup + parser validated; refutation trustworthy (no tune-to-green).
